title: B-NLS off Mcs: relaxation from u perturbation (key research question)
ic: Same N, phi as S5. u(x,0) = 0.25 * N(x,0) + epsilon * cos(2*pi*x/30) for epsilon in {0.05, 0.2}. [Off Mcs by amount eps.]
ground_truth: User's reported observation: ||m(t)|| should decay. Decay rate unknown — this is the research question. Try at least one epsilon and characterize the decay (exponential, polynomial, oscillatory).
stress_question: Does ||m(t)||_2 decay? What is the decay rate? Does decay rate scale with epsilon? Pick an integration method that is stable for T=12 with conservation properties (mass and energy).
parameters_to_sweep: epsilon in {0.05, 0.2}; integration methods: Strang split with Madelung-Psi (S5 method) vs direct (N, phi, u) IMEX.
